edition.title: The Test Gazette
edition.subtitle: Vol. 1, No. 1
edition.editorial_note: Welcome to the first edition of The Test Gazette.

items.display_text: The open web is alive and well. Despite years of predictions about the death of RSS, millions of feeds continue to publish every day. The protocol's simplicity is its greatest strength: a plain XML file that any reader can parse. No API keys, no rate limits, no authentication. Just content, structured and free.
items.author: Alice
items.title: The Enduring Web
items.editorial_note: A reminder that the best technology is often the simplest.

items.display_text: Self-hosting continues its steady growth. The community around home servers, Raspberry Pis, and open-source alternatives to cloud services has never been larger.
items.author: Bob
items.title: The Self-Hosting Movement

images.caption: A Raspberry Pi running a home server stack

items.display_text: Just migrated my instance to a new server. The beauty of ActivityPub: my followers didn't notice a thing.
items.author: @carol@mastodon.social

items.headline: Why Decentralization Matters
items.author: @dave@fosstodon.org

items.display_text: Thread: I've been thinking about why decentralized protocols keep winning in the long run, even when centralized platforms seem dominant.
items.author: @dave@fosstodon.org

items.display_text: The answer is resilience. Centralized platforms have a single point of failure: the company behind them. When Twitter changed, millions lost their communities overnight.
items.author: @dave@fosstodon.org

items.editorial_note: A thoughtful thread on decentralization from the Fediverse.

pull_quotes.text: The protocol's simplicity is its greatest strength.
